headline: Visible dispersion, within-species spatial climatic association and among-species environmental sorting are distinct structures; no common cross-module coupling remained after slope uncertainty was modelled.

balanced_image_comparison_atlas.purpose: visible variance partitioning, species-level PCA, among-species summaries and historical sensitivity

exhaustive_detected_layer.purpose: post-detection source layer before coordinate and spatial filtering

exhaustive_spatially_thinned_primary.purpose: primary within-species climate coefficients and precision-aware cross-species analysis

revised_precision_aware_cohort.module_weighting: equal orientation, colour and shape weights

legacy_lability_cohort.status: withdrawn as primary evidence after precision audit

legacy_precision_audit.decision: withdraw the negative-correlation and median-split quadrant claims

exhaustive_spatially_thinned_primary_36_.allowed_claim: Four non-circular linear endpoint-climate rows passed BH correction and were small; four hue-component rows also passed component-wise correction but require joint circular interpretation.

balanced_atlas_le_10km_sensitivity.allowed_claim: No main endpoint-predictor row passed BH correction in the smaller balanced-atlas <=10 km sensitivity.

within_species_inference_levels.interpretation_rule: The exhaustive 46,276-observation primary cohort and the earlier balanced-atlas sensitivities are different datasets and must never share an FDR label.
